# opnDossier

> Firewall Configuration Processor - Transform OPNsense and pfSense configuration files into documentation, machine-readable exports (JSON/YAML/HTML/Markdown/text), and security audit reports. Offline-first, no telemetry, works in airgapped environments.

This file is a repo-orientation primer for AI coding assistants (Copilot, Cursor-style IDE assistants, Claude-in-repo) that have cloned or indexed this repository. It describes the codebase layout, tech stack, and contributor-facing docs.

If you are an AI agent **invoking the `opnDossier` CLI at runtime** (not contributing to it), the better starting point is [docs/for-agents.md](docs/for-agents.md). If you are an LLM crawler fetching the published docs site, the conventional entry point is [https://opndossier.com/llms.txt](https://opndossier.com/llms.txt) (this file's spec-compliant sibling served at the site root).

## Key Characteristics

- **Offline-first** — no network calls at runtime, designed for airgapped environments
- **No telemetry** — zero data collection or external communication
- **Multi-format export** — Markdown, JSON, YAML, HTML, plain text; every format is serialized from the same `pkg/model.CommonDevice` domain model
- **Honest-empty-field policy** — subsystems not yet implemented for a device type emit a stable `"not yet implemented in <device> converter"` ConversionWarning substring so automation can distinguish product gaps from missing configuration; canonical list: `pkg/parser/pfsense.KnownGaps()`
- **Public Go API** — `pkg/parser`, `pkg/model`, `pkg/schema/*` are semver-committed from v1.5; compile-time shape tests + `go doc -all` goldies guard against drift
- **Security analysis** — vulnerability detection, dead-rule analysis, unused-resource analysis
- **Compliance auditing** — built-in STIG, SANS, and firewall-baseline plugins; third-party compliance plugins loadable as `.so` files via `--plugin-dir` (Linux/macOS/FreeBSD only, preflight-hardened)
- **Configuration sanitization** — field-pattern and value-detector redaction of credentials, keys, and community strings
- **Configuration diffing** — compare two configs with security-aware change analysis
- **Go 1.26+** — single static binary for Linux / macOS / Windows

## Project Structure

- `cmd/` — CLI command entry points (Cobra-based, wrapped with `charmbracelet/fang`)
- `pkg/` — **Public Go API** (semver-committed from v1.5)
  - `pkg/model/` — Platform-agnostic `CommonDevice` domain model plus `ConversionWarning`, `Severity`, `DeviceType`, firewall/NAT/DHCP/VPN subsystem structs
  - `pkg/parser/` — `Factory`, `OPNsenseXMLDecoder` interface, `DeviceParser` interface, `DeviceParserRegistry`
    - `pkg/parser/opnsense/` — OPNsense `Parser` and `ConvertDocument(*schema.OpnSenseDocument)`; self-registers via `init()`
    - `pkg/parser/pfsense/` — pfSense `Parser` and `ConvertDocument(*pfschema.Document)`; self-registers via `init()`; has its own XML decoder because the shared `OPNsenseXMLDecoder` is OPNsense-typed
  - `pkg/schema/` — XML DTO layer (tracks vendor upstream, public but vendor-tracking)
    - `pkg/schema/opnsense/` — `OpnSenseDocument` and nested OPNsense XML DTOs
    - `pkg/schema/pfsense/` — `Document` and pfSense-specific DTOs (forked copy-on-write from OPNsense where they diverge)
    - `pkg/schema/shared/` — cross-platform helper types (`FlexBool`, `FlexInt`, shared DHCP/Unbound structs)
- `internal/` — **Private application logic** (not importable by external consumers)
  - `internal/analysis/` — Shared analysis + canonical finding types, detection functions, statistics
  - `internal/audit/` — Audit engine, compliance plugin registry/manager, dynamic plugin loader + preflight
  - `internal/cfgparser/` — `XMLParser` implementation of `parser.OPNsenseXMLDecoder` (charset detection, XXE protection, size limits)
  - `internal/compliance/` — Plugin interfaces and canonical `Finding` type
  - `internal/config/` — Viper-based configuration management
  - `internal/constants/` — Shared constants and validation whitelists
  - `internal/converter/` — Data conversion and report generation
    - `internal/converter/builder/` — Programmatic markdown builder and streaming writer
    - `internal/converter/formatters/` — Format-specific renderers (JSON, YAML, HTML, text)
  - `internal/diff/` — Configuration diff engine with security-aware change analysis
  - `internal/display/` — Terminal display formatting
  - `internal/docgen/` — Cobra-doc generation helpers
  - `internal/export/` — File export with overwrite protection
  - `internal/logging/` — `charmbracelet/log` wrapper with `IsVerbose()` gate for sensitive diagnostics
  - `internal/plugins/` — Built-in compliance plugins (`firewall/`, `sans/`, `stig/`)
  - `internal/pool/` — Worker pool for concurrent multi-file processing
  - `internal/processor/` — Data processing and report generation pipeline
  - `internal/progress/` — CLI progress indicators (spinner, bar)
  - `internal/sanitizer/` — Sanitization engine (field-pattern + value-detector rules, XML streaming + struct reflection paths)
  - `internal/validator/` — Semantic validation rules injected into `pkg/parser/*` via `SetValidator`
- `docs/` — Documentation site (mkdocs)
  - `docs/for-agents.md` — Runtime-invocation entry point for AI agents and automation pipelines
  - `docs/llms.txt` — llmstxt.org-spec-compliant site-root summary (served at `https://opndossier.com/llms.txt`)
  - `docs/cli/` — Cobra-auto-generated exhaustive flag reference (regenerated by `just generate-cli-docs`)
  - `docs/development/` — Architecture, API reference, public-API contract, contributing, releasing
  - `docs/user-guide/` — Hand-written operator narrative for each command
  - `docs/data-model/` — `CommonDevice` schema reference and JSON/YAML export examples
  - `docs/security/` — NIST IR 7608-style security assurance case
- `project_spec/` — Project specifications (requirements, user stories, tasks)
- `testdata/` — Shared test fixtures

## Technology Stack

- CLI framework: `github.com/spf13/cobra`, wrapped with `github.com/charmbracelet/fang`
- Terminal styling: `github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss`, `github.com/charmbracelet/glamour`
- Markdown generation: `github.com/nao1215/markdown`
- Markdown-to-HTML: `github.com/yuin/goldmark` (+ `goldmark-emoji`)
- Configuration: `github.com/spf13/viper`
- Logging: `github.com/charmbracelet/log`
- Data formats: `encoding/xml`, `encoding/json`, `gopkg.in/yaml.v3`

## Documentation (authoritative sources, read before making changes)

- [README](https://github.com/EvilBit-Labs/opnDossier/blob/main/README.md) — Project overview, install, quick start
- [AGENTS.md](https://github.com/EvilBit-Labs/opnDossier/blob/main/AGENTS.md) — Agent-specific behavioral rules and precedence
- [GOTCHAS.md](https://github.com/EvilBit-Labs/opnDossier/blob/main/GOTCHAS.md) — Non-obvious behaviors, pitfalls, hard-won lessons (read this before touching tests, plugins, or XML parsing)
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/EvilBit-Labs/opnDossier/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) — PR process, commit conventions, testing expectations
- [Architecture Overview](https://github.com/EvilBit-Labs/opnDossier/blob/main/docs/development/architecture.md) — System design and component interactions
- [Development Standards](https://github.com/EvilBit-Labs/opnDossier/blob/main/docs/development/standards.md) — Go patterns, implementation details, data processing
- [Public API Contract](https://github.com/EvilBit-Labs/opnDossier/blob/main/docs/development/public-api.md) — Semver policy and stable surface
- [Plugin Development](https://github.com/EvilBit-Labs/opnDossier/blob/main/docs/development/plugin-development.md) — Compliance plugin and device parser authoring
- [Requirements](https://github.com/EvilBit-Labs/opnDossier/blob/main/project_spec/requirements.md) — Project requirements and specifications
- [Releasing](https://github.com/EvilBit-Labs/opnDossier/blob/main/RELEASING.md) — Release process and signing
- [Security Policy](https://github.com/EvilBit-Labs/opnDossier/blob/main/SECURITY.md) — Vulnerability disclosure policy

## Commands

```text
# Convert / display
opnDossier display config.xml                               # Display config in terminal (Markdown + theme)
opnDossier convert config.xml                               # Convert to Markdown (stdout)
opnDossier convert config.xml -f json -o output.json        # Export to JSON
opnDossier convert config.xml -f yaml -o output.yaml        # Export to YAML
opnDossier convert config.xml -f html -o output.html        # Export to self-contained HTML

# Validate / audit / diff
opnDossier validate config.xml                              # Structural + semantic validation
opnDossier audit config.xml                                 # Run blue-mode compliance audit (default)
opnDossier audit config.xml --mode red                      # Attacker-focused recon (experimental; stub output)
opnDossier audit config.xml --plugins stig,sans             # Run a subset of built-in plugins
opnDossier audit config.xml --plugin-dir /opt/plugins       # Load third-party .so plugins (POSIX only)
opnDossier diff old.xml new.xml                             # Compare two configs with security-aware analysis

# Sanitize (redact sensitive values into a new file)
opnDossier sanitize config.xml --mode aggressive -o safe.xml   # --mode: conservative | moderate | aggressive

# Config file / completions / docs
opnDossier config init                                      # Emit a fully annotated default config
opnDossier config show                                      # Show effective merged config
opnDossier config validate                                  # Validate the config file
opnDossier completion bash|zsh|fish|powershell              # Shell completion scripts
opnDossier man ./man                                        # Generate man pages
```

Device type is auto-detected from the XML root element (`<opnsense>` vs `<pfsense>`). Force it with the global `--device-type` flag if needed.

## Development

```text
just dev *args          # Run opnDossier locally (alias for `just run`)
just build              # Build the binary [alias: b]
just build-release      # Build with optimizations for release
just test               # Run all tests [alias: t]
just test-race          # Run tests with race detector (locally enforced; not in CI)
just test-integration   # Run integration tests (build tag)
just test-coverage      # Generate coverage profile
just coverage           # Run tests and open coverage HTML in browser
just lint               # Run golangci-lint via mise-pinned toolchain
just ci-check           # Full CI-equivalent checks (pre-commit + format + lint + test + integration + race)
just ci-smoke           # Fast smoke tests (minimal validation)
just scan               # Run gosec security scanner
just generate-cli-docs  # Regenerate docs/cli/opnDossier_*.md from Cobra definitions
just docs-build         # Build the mkdocs documentation site
```

`just ci-check` is the pre-commit authority: run it before any commit that will be pushed. A test, lint, or format failure there is the same failure CI will hit.
